title: Gentle Fermented Egyptian Shrimp Stew
description: A comforting and nutritious Egyptian dish featuring fermented shrimp paste, perfect for a sick day, made with katsuramuki-cut vegetables and cooked in an earthenware bowl.
instructions: Cook the shrimp in boiling water for 2 minutes, then set aside.
Heat the olive oil in the earthenware bowl over medium heat.
Add the katsuramuki-cut onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the fermented saeujeot-shrimp-paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the cooked shrimp and season with salt and black pepper.
Simmer for an additional 2 minutes and serve hot.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
